prediction.summary: A credential stuffing attack targeting the 'user@corp' account was detected, involving multiple failed login attempts from various source IPs across both SSH and web login services. The attack culminated in a successful login to the 'user@corp' account from IP 198.51.100.123, indicating a confirmed account compromise. An audit alert further confirmed distributed credential reuse, highlighting the severity of the incident.

timeline.event: Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.12
Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.33
Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.44
Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.88
Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.101
Successful SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.123
Audit alert: Distributed credential reuse detected across 8 source IPs for account user@corp

recommendations: Immediately disable the 'user@corp' account and force a password reset. Revoke all active sessions for this user.
Block the source IP 198.51.100.123 at the firewall or security group to prevent further access.
Investigate post-login activity for 'user@corp' from 198.51.100.123 to determine the extent of compromise, any lateral movement, or data exfiltration.
Review MFA logs for 'user@corp' to determine if MFA was bypassed or not enforced for the successful login.
Implement or enhance rate-limiting (e.g., fail2ban) on SSH and web login endpoints to mitigate brute force and credential stuffing attempts.
